query: Give this design doc a brutal, cynical review and tell me everything wrong with it.
Be skeptical and tear apart my feature spec — what's missing, what's weak?
I want a harsh critical review of this proposal before I send it.
Adversarially review this document and list the problems.
Walk every branch and boundary condition in this function and list only the unhandled edge cases.
Run code review on my staged changes and triage the findings into patch/defer buckets.
Does this zero-egress proof harness actually prove what it claims, or is the green fake?
